message:
fix(tools): correct trailing-newline semantics in truncation and add DoS guard

Fix incorrect line-counting behavior in truncate_head() and
truncate_tail() where a trailing newline was counted as starting a new
empty line. A file containing "a\n" is 1 line (terminated), not 2 lines
(with an empty second line). This affected truncation thresholds and
metadata reporting.

truncate_head() changes:
- Empty string now correctly reports 0 total lines (was 1).
- Trailing newline no longer inflates line count.

truncate_tail() complete rewrite:
- Backward search loop rewritten for correct line boundary detection.
- Zero-line budget now returns empty content with correct metadata.
- Byte-limit partial output fallback path fixed.
- Trailing newline handling matches truncate_head() semantics.

Additional changes:
- Add BASH_FILE_LIMIT_BYTES constant (100MB) for bash output DoS
  protection — prevents tools from reading unbounded output into memory.
- Add #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)] where needed.

Test updates:
- truncate_tail_empty_content: total_lines 1→0.
- truncate_tail_file_ending_with_newline: flip assertion (no truncation).
- truncate_tail_max_lines_zero: add content/byte assertions.
- New proptest regression seed for single-byte replacement char edge case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
